items.stem: The stray dog was famished, so it gobbled up the food at once. 'Famished' means ___.
items.rationale: Gobbling up the food at once tells us the dog was very hungry.

items.stem: The room was immaculate — not a speck of dust anywhere. 'Immaculate' means ___.
items.rationale: 'Not a speck of dust' shows the room was very clean.

items.stem: He felt drowsy and could hardly keep his eyes open. 'Drowsy' means ___.
items.rationale: Hardly keeping his eyes open shows he felt sleepy.

items.stem: The soup was scalding, so she waited for it to cool. 'Scalding' means ___.
items.rationale: Waiting for it to cool shows the soup was very hot.

items.stem: The instructions were baffling and no one understood them. 'Baffling' means ___.
items.rationale: 'No one understood them' shows the instructions were confusing.

items.stem: The market was bustling with shoppers on Sunday morning. 'Bustling' means ___.
items.rationale: Full of shoppers shows the market was very busy.

items.stem: The old bridge was rickety and shook as we crossed. 'Rickety' means ___.
items.rationale: Shaking as they crossed shows the bridge was unsteady.

items.stem: She was elated when she won first prize. 'Elated' means ___.
items.rationale: Winning first prize would make her very happy.

items.stem: The detective examined every clue meticulously. 'Meticulously' means ___.
items.rationale: A detective checking every clue would work very carefully.

items.stem: The desert was arid, with no water for miles. 'Arid' means ___.
items.rationale: 'No water for miles' shows the desert was very dry.

items.stem: The naughty boy was scolded for his mischief. 'Mischief' means ___.
items.rationale: Being scolded and 'naughty' show mischief means bad behaviour.

items.stem: The runner was exhausted and collapsed at the finish line. 'Exhausted' means ___.
items.rationale: Collapsing at the finish line shows the runner was very tired.

items.stem: The teacher's voice was audible even at the back of the hall. 'Audible' means ___.
items.rationale: Heard even at the back means the voice was able to be heard.

items.stem: The generous man donated all his savings to charity. 'Generous' means ___.
items.rationale: Donating all his savings shows the man was giving.

items.stem: The cave was pitch-dark and we needed a torch. 'Pitch-dark' means ___.
items.rationale: Needing a torch shows the cave was completely dark.

items.stem: The crowd was jubilant when the team scored. 'Jubilant' means ___.
items.rationale: Cheering as the team scored shows the crowd was very joyful.

items.stem: The medicine was foul and she made a face as she swallowed it. 'Foul' means ___.
items.rationale: Making a face shows the medicine tasted very unpleasant.

items.stem: The path was treacherous after the heavy rain. 'Treacherous' means ___.
items.rationale: A slippery path after heavy rain would be dangerous.

items.stem: He spoke in a feeble voice because he was very weak. 'Feeble' means ___.
items.rationale: 'Because he was very weak' tells us feeble means weak.

items.stem: The novel was captivating and she read it in one sitting. 'Captivating' means ___.
items.rationale: Reading it in one sitting shows the novel was very interesting.

items.stem: The soldiers were vigilant and watched the gate all night. 'Vigilant' means ___.
items.rationale: Watching the gate all night shows they were watchful.

items.stem: The frail old lady needed help to climb the stairs. 'Frail' means ___.
items.rationale: Needing help to climb stairs shows she was weak.

items.stem: The classroom was in chaos before the teacher came in. 'Chaos' means ___.
items.rationale: A wild classroom before the teacher came shows disorder.

items.stem: The timid kitten hid whenever guests came to visit. 'Timid' means ___.
items.rationale: Hiding from guests shows the kitten was shy.

VOC-context.iCanStatement: I can work out what a hard word means from the sentence.
VOC-context.explanation: When you meet a word you do not know, read the rest of the sentence for clues. What someone does or how something behaves often tells you the meaning without a dictionary.

workedExamples.text: He was famished, so he ate everything.
workedExamples.note: Eating everything is the clue — 'famished' means very hungry.

workedExamples.text: The room was immaculate, with no dust.
workedExamples.note: 'No dust' tells you 'immaculate' means very clean.
